Advocates for affordable health care, employment and income security, and protection from financial abuse. 1455 SW Broadway Suite 1490 Portland, OR 97201 Eligibility: Unrestricted, but targeted towards age 50 and older Hours: National office: Monday-Friday 4am-8pm Oregon office: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (866) 554-5360 - Get Directions Visit Website More Details ALZHEIMER'S ASSOCIATION OREGON ALZHEIMER'S ASSOCIATION CENTRAL OREGON 24/7 HELPLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides care and support for those affected by Alzheimer's and other dementias. Funds, promotes, and supports Alzheimer's research aimed at treatment, prevention, and a cure. Also operates an informational helpline and provides information and referral services. 1080 SW Mount Bachelor Drive #100 Bend, OR 97702 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (800) 272-3900 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ALZHEIMER'S ASSOCIATION OREGON ALZHEIMER'S ASSOCIATION MID-WILLAMETTE VALLEY 24/7 HELPLINE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides care and support for those affected by Alzheimer's and other dementias. Also operates an informational helpline and provides information and referral services. 5285 Meadows Road Suite 451 Lake Oswego, OR 97035 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (800) 272-3900 247 , Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIONAL COUNCIL ON AGING BENEFITSCHECKUP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The BenefitsCheckup assists in determining eligibility for food, prescription, health care, utility assistance, and other benefit programs 251 18th Street S Suite 500 Arlington, VA 22202 Eligibility: Age 60 and older and adults with disabilities Hours: Office hours: Monday-Friday 5am-4pm Email (800) 794-6559 Get Directions Visit Website More Details USAGING ELDERCARE LOCATOR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with locating services and advocating for older adults. The Eldercare locator database is designed to link older adults and their caregivers to unbiased information and assistance resources at the state and community level.
